pattern: Sliding a window across the array while tracking which values it currently holds turns 'longest unique-valued stretch' into a single left-to-right sweep instead of checking every stretch separately.
hints: Grow a window from the left; whenever the newly-included value already sits inside the window, that is the signal something has to leave, not that the whole window is invalid.
Shrink from the window's left edge, removing values (and subtracting them from a running sum) one at a time until the duplicate is gone, then the window is unique again.
Keep a running sum of the current window and a best-so-far, updating the best every time the window grows, since the window is always unique by construction.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, best subarray is a suffix
statement example, two subarrays tie for best sum
single element at the maximum value
every value identical, best is just one of them
already all unique, the whole array is the answer
duplicate right at the start forces an early shrink
duplicate value far apart still forces a shrink when reached

stress.description: random values from a small alphabet so duplicates are frequent, kept modest so the O(n^2) oracle stays fast
stress.note: kept at n<=2000 so the O(n^2) oracle finishes quickly
